# Attestation Report — 2026-03-01

**Scope:** Full specification (21 modules, 181 active requirements)
**Generated by:** Recompiled from 21 individual attestation files (dated 2026-02-21)
**Note:** No commits have been made since the previous attestation run (2026-02-21). All individual attestation files in `docs/4-docs/attestations/` remain current. This report recompiles from the same data with an updated date.

---

## Executive Summary

| Verdict | Count | % |
|---------|-------|---|
| CONFORMANT | 35 | 19.3% |
| PARTIAL | 69 | 38.1% |
| NON-CONFORMANT | 18 | 9.9% |
| NOT ASSESSED | 59 | 32.6% |

The system's core v1 feature set -- Jake daemon, mortal spawning, chat switching, commitment verification, doc store, logging, dependency injection, and quality infrastructure -- is solid. Conformant requirements cluster in the infrastructure and process layers (quality, architecture, agents). The large NOT ASSESSED bloc (33%) is expected: sandbox, workflows, servitor trees, and several other modules are explicitly deferred for post-v1.

The significant gap territory is NON-CONFORMANT must-have requirements in the states/modes (019), capability delegation (021), and lifecycle (006) modules, which together represent foundational multi-agent infrastructure that has not yet been built.

## Top Gaps (Highest Impact)

Gaps are ordered by priority (must-have first) then by module number. Focus is on must-have requirements that are NON-CONFORMANT or have critical unsatisfied properties.

### 1. REQ-STM-002/003/004/005/006 -- Three Orthogonal Properties + Inactivity Timeout (019)

**Module:** 019-states-modes | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT (5 reqs)

No implementation of any kind. `Mortal` and `Jake` have no `backgrounding`, `perseverance`, or `userPresence` boolean properties. `MortalSpawner.summon()` has no parameters for these modes. No inactivity timer or configurable timeout exists. No prod/reap action on timeout. These five requirements form the foundational operating-mode layer that 007, 009, and 020 all depend on.

**Systemic impact:** Operating modes (007), communication routing (009), and servitor tree mode inheritance (020) are all blocked on this infrastructure.

### 2. REQ-CAP-001/002/004/006 -- Capability Delegation Core Architecture (021)

**Module:** 021-capability-delegation | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT (4 reqs)

No capability delegation system exists. `summon_servitor` returns a plain text UUID string, not a typed promise handle. No `delegate(handle, capabilities)` operation exists anywhere. No `Summoned` state (awaiting capabilities). `Servitor.sessionMode` is publicly mutable -- agents *can* modify their own capabilities, which is the opposite of REQ-CAP-006. The `PermissionManager` system handles single-session tool approval only, not inter-agent capability delegation.

**Systemic impact:** Servitor trees (020 REQ-TRE-004, REQ-TRE-006) cannot be completed until capability delegation exists.

### 3. REQ-LCM-001 -- Fish-or-Cut-Bait Triggers (006)

**Module:** 006-lifecycle | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT

Zero implementation. No token tracking, no changeset validation, no spin counting, no gang-level abort, no self-termination protocol. `ServitorState` has no `failed` or `reaped` cases. No parent notification on trigger. The five trigger conditions (token budget, wrong changeset, spinning, whole gang wrong, servitor self-abort) are entirely undetectable.

### 4. REQ-LCM-002 -- Token Budget Enforcement (006)

**Module:** 006-lifecycle | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT

No token budget parameter in `MortalSpawner.summon()` or `Mortal.init`. `QueryOptions` is constructed with no token limit. Agents can consume unbounded tokens.

**Systemic impact:** REQ-TRE-005 (Token Budget Inheritance) cannot be built without this.

### 5. REQ-LCM-003 -- Spin Detection (006)

**Module:** 006-lifecycle | **Priority:** should-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT

No spin detection logic. Would require intercepting tool call sequences from streaming SDK events; streaming events are forwarded through `Mortal.sendStreaming` but no sequence analysis is performed.

### 6. REQ-AGT-006 -- Task Modes (004)

**Module:** 004-agents | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT

No `TaskMode` type exists. No execute/delegate/plan/break-up/unify modes. No mode transitions, no mode observability, no queryable current mode. `PermissionMode` (`.plan`, `.normal`, etc.) is a Claude permission mode, not a task mode -- these are entirely distinct concepts. The spec itself flags this as needing further design.

### 7. REQ-COM-001 -- Upward Bubbling (009)

**Module:** 009-communication | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NOT ASSESSED (no implementation)

No message routing infrastructure for child-to-parent escalation. Jake has no inbound relay path from child servitors. No delivery guarantee. A message from a Mortal cannot reach the user through any intermediary chain.

### 8. REQ-COM-004 -- Question Classification (009)

**Module:** 009-communication | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NOT ASSESSED (no implementation)

No question classification system. Questions reach the user through Jake's chat without any pre-classification. No quick/deep distinction modeled. No classification conveyed in notifications.

### 9. REQ-COM-006 -- Message Protocol Expectations (009)

**Module:** 009-communication | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NOT ASSESSED (spec-pinned)

No typed message protocol exists. Jake and Mortal communicate via unstructured `String` payloads. No protocol-level timeout or response-type validation. Spec's own open questions section acknowledges this is "pinned for future design."

### 10. REQ-INV-001 -- Test Suite Gate (002)

**Module:** 002-invariants | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NOT ASSESSED

No CI/CD pipeline exists. No `.github/workflows/` or equivalent. Tests pass locally (`redo Tavern/test`) but nothing enforces this at merge time. The gate exists as a stated policy only, with no automated enforcement.

### 11. REQ-INV-008/009 -- Original File Protection + Sandbox Boundary Impossibility (002)

**Module:** 002-invariants | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NOT ASSESSED (2 reqs)

No changeset/overlay system. Agents work directly on files. App explicitly has `com.apple.security.app-sandbox: false`, which is the opposite of sandbox boundary enforcement. Both are deferred per spec with explicit deferral notes.

### 12. REQ-INV-007 -- Visible Failures (002) -- Silent Catch Blocks

**Module:** 002-invariants | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** PARTIAL (critical property unsatisfied)

Three confirmed silent failure violations:

- `SessionStore.swift:168-170` -- `catch { // Silent failure }` in `saveServitorList()`
- `SessionStore.swift:181-183` -- silent `return []` on decode failure in `loadServitorList()`
- `ProjectView.swift:131,138` -- `print("Failed to spawn/close servitor: \(error)")` -- console only, not observable

### 13. REQ-CDS-002 -- Underlying Sessions (022)

**Module:** 022-chat-discussions | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT

Strictly one-to-one session-per-servitor. No multi-session tracking. No session transition logging. If a session expires and is replaced, the old history is lost. Session corruption surfaces as an error offering `startFreshSession()` -- which starts clean with no prior context.

### 14. REQ-CDS-004 -- Session Recreation with Context Injection (022)

**Module:** 022-chat-discussions | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT

`startFreshSession()` clears error state but injects no prior context into the new session's system prompt. Users must re-explain context after session recreation.

### 15. REQ-OBS-001/002/004 -- Metrics Infrastructure (015)

**Module:** 015-observability | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NOT ASSESSED (3 reqs)

Zero implementation across three must-have metrics requirements. No `tokenTime`, `toolTime`, `wallClockTime`, `throttleTime` tracking. No utilization or saturation computation. No amplification factor. These are foundational requirements with no deferred status, yet no metrics infrastructure exists in the codebase.

### 16. REQ-LCM-004 -- Dead Agent Sidebar Persistence (006)

**Module:** 006-lifecycle | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** PARTIAL (key property unsatisfied)

`dismiss()` removes the mortal from `ServitorRegistry`, so `ServitorListViewModel.refreshItems()` drops it from the sidebar. The spec requires dead agents to remain visible. No "tombstone" list exists alongside the live registry.

### 17. REQ-LCM-007 -- Jake Completion Notification (006)

**Module:** 006-lifecycle | **Priority:** should-have | **Verdict:** PARTIAL (must-have property unsatisfied)

When a Mortal reaches `.done`, nothing informs Jake or `TavernCoordinator`. No observation/callback path from Mortal state changes upward. `ServitorListViewModel` only refreshes on explicit `servitorsDidChange()` calls -- it does not reactively observe Mortal state transitions.

### 18. REQ-OBS-005 -- Boundary Attempt Reporting (015)

**Module:** 015-observability | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** PARTIAL (key properties unsatisfied)

`PermissionManager` intercepts tool use as a pre-approval gate, but: (a) rules are global, not per-agent or per-project; (b) `PermissionDecision` is binary allow/deny -- no pause/reap/warn options; (c) boundary violations are logged to `TavernLogger.permissions` only -- no notification to parent agent; (d) the implementation is a tool-gating approval flow, not a boundary-attempt audit log.

### 19. REQ-INV-005 -- Doc Store as Source of Truth (002)

**Module:** 002-invariants | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** PARTIAL (write-through gap)

Session IDs persist in UserDefaults (not doc store), which is a deliberate architectural split. The more critical gap: `SessionStore.saveServitorList()` has a silent catch block (line 168-170) -- persistence failures are swallowed. No end-to-end restart test verifying state restoration from disk alone.

### 20. REQ-QA-018 -- Grade 2.9 Local LLM Testing (016)

**Module:** 016-quality | **Priority:** must-have | **Verdict:** NON-CONFORMANT

No `TavernLocalLLMTests` target. No `redo Tavern/test-grade2.9` script. No local LLM testing infrastructure of any kind. The spec notes "TBD on exact setup," acknowledging this is forward-looking, but the requirement is active and unimplemented.

---

## Systemic Themes

**Theme 1 -- Multi-agent infrastructure not yet built.** Modules 019 (states/modes), 021 (capability delegation), 020 (servitor trees), and 009 (communication) together define the Tavern's multi-agent coordination model. None is implemented beyond stubs. These form a dependency chain: states/modes -> capability delegation -> servitor trees -> cross-tree communication.

**Theme 2 -- Silent failures violate a core invariant.** REQ-INV-007 (Visible Failures) is violated in three confirmed locations in `SessionStore` and `ProjectView`. These are not edge cases -- they are in the persistence hot path.

**Theme 3 -- Provenance coverage is partial.** REQ-FID-002 shows 58% of source files lack provenance marks. Grade 3 integration tests (XCTestCase) have no `.tags()` mechanism, breaking traceability for the most authoritative test grade. Seven REQ-FID requirements are all PARTIAL.

**Theme 4 -- Metrics infrastructure entirely absent.** REQ-OBS-001 through REQ-OBS-004 are must-have requirements with no deferred status, yet no metrics infrastructure -- token time, utilization, saturation, amplification -- exists anywhere in the codebase.

**Theme 5 -- Deferred features are correctly NOT ASSESSED.** Sandbox (011), workflows (012), servitor trees (020), and v1-deferred items in 017 account for most of the NOT ASSESSED bloc. This is architecturally correct -- these are explicitly post-v1.

---
